action.hypothesis: The last round showed the gated low-K capacity cut was a real quality regression, and the search has now gone several rounds without advancing an incubating alternative family. The least-bad informative move is to get a real proxy read on the already-implemented `group_topk` family at K=128 under the strongest current training regime, removing the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` override that caused the earlier policy crashes. This will finally test whether local winner-take-all competition can beat other non-gated support selectors on anchor-quality rather than being discarded for an execution-layer mismatch.
action.summary: Run the first real proxy comparison for `group_topk` at K=128 using the current strongest gated training regime, but without the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` env override that caused the earlier crashes.
action.self_review: This advances an incubating family instead of spending another round on weak local gated tuning, and it specifically resolves prior `group_topk` crashes as execution-layer issues rather than architectural evidence. The main risk is that hard local competition may still be too restrictive to challenge gated on FVU, but one clean proxy run is necessary before deciding whether to retire or stabilize this family.
notes_to_memory: Rounds 32-33 did not provide architectural evidence on `group_topk`; they were rejected because `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` is not an allowed execution-layer override.
This round retries `group_topk` with only allowed overrides so the family gets its first actual proxy measurement under the current strongest K=128 training regime.
Using the gated anchor settings at K=128 isolates architecture as the primary changed variable and gives a clean read on whether local competition is a viable quality direction.
next_hypotheses: If `group_topk` is competitive with gated at K=128, keep the family alive and follow with a code-round that softens or enriches the grouped selection mechanism inside `sparsify/`, since group-size sweeps are not exposed by the execution layer.

If `group_topk` is clearly worse on quality under a normal run, retire hard local grouping and move to a more expressive multi-branch or factorized encoder family instead of more support-ranking variants.
If `group_topk` is mediocre on FVU but materially better on runtime or memory, evaluate it as a cost-quality tradeoff family before discarding it.
